is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study examines associations between a+-thalassemia and Plasmodium falciparum malarial infection in Tanzania.

reports_target_markers.evidence: The paper studies human genetic markers (a3.7 thalassemia deletion, HbS sickle cell, and 15 autosomal STR loci). No P. falciparum drug-resistance markers (Pfcrt, Pfdhfr, Pfdhps, Pfk13, Pfmdr1, Pfcytb) are reported.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Frequencies reported are for human hemoglobin genotypes/alleles, not parasite drug-resistance codons.

sub_country_location.evidence: Data reported by 13 named villages in Kilimanjaro and Tanga regions (Table 1), but this applies to human genetic data, not parasite markers.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Cross-sectional surveys conducted in October and November 2001.

african_field_samples.evidence: Field samples collected from children in villages in northeastern Tanzania.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Conducted under the Joint Malaria Programme; data for Mgome derived from Lusingu et al. [19] and other villages from Drakeley et al. [17].

supplement.evidence: No relevant drug-resistance marker data in supplement; the study concerns human genetics.

assessment.summary: This study investigates associations between human hemoglobinopathies (a+-thalassemia a3.7 deletion and sickle cell HbS) and P. falciparum malaria transmission intensity across 13 villages in northeastern Tanzania. It reports human genetic allele frequencies, not any P. falciparum drug-resistance marker frequencies.
exclusion_reasons: No curated P. falciparum drug-resistance markers reported; the study concerns human host genetic markers (a-thalassemia, HbS, autosomal STRs) rather than parasite resistance codons.
No extractable per-locus frequencies for any target drug-resistance marker.
